title: 10 best herbal tinctures for eye swelling

remedies_list.remedy_desc: American witch hazel tinctures helps with eye swelling because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The tincture contains a compound called gallic acid, which is known for its ability to reduce inflammation and ease discomfort in the eyes. When applied topically or taken as a supplement, witch hazel tincture can help constrict blood vessels and reduce fluid buildup, resulting in a significant decrease in eye swelling. This natural remedy can provide fast and effective relief from puffy, swollen eyes caused by allergies, sinus pressure, or other conditions.
remedy_properties: Astringent properties: The tannins present in Witch Hazel help constrict blood vessels, reducing inflammation and swelling around the eyes by limiting fluid leakage.
Antioxidant activity: The antioxidant compounds in Witch Hazel, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to eye inflammation and swelling.

Anti-inflammatory effects: The bioactive compounds in Witch Hazel, including lignans and triterpenoids, exhibit anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce redness, itching, and swelling associated with eye issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, witch hazel roots have been traditionally used to reduce inflammation and alleviate eye discomfort.
Buds: The young buds contain a high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids, which help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh witch hazel leaves hamamelis virginiana during full moon.
Chop the witch hazel leaves into small pieces weighing about 20 ounces.
Combine chopped witch hazel with 80 proof vodka at a ratio of 1:4.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for six weeks steeping slowly.
Strain and filter the tincture using cheesecloth or a coffee filter discarding solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tinctures helps with eye swelling because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, specifically targeting the delicate tissues surrounding the eyes. The tannins present in the herb have been shown to reduce puffiness and redness by constricting blood vessels and reducing fluid accumulation. Additionally, pot marigold's antioxidant properties help protect the eyes from further damage caused by free radicals. As a result, this natural remedy can effectively alleviate eye swelling and promote a more refreshed and youthful appearance.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Calendula's flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, have been shown to reduce inflammation by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thus helping to alleviate eye swelling.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of Pot Marigold tincture help to neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and contribute to eye swelling, promoting healthy tissue function and reducing damage to the eyes.

Vasodilatory: Calendula's flavonoids also exhibit vasodilatory effects, which means they help to dilate blood vessels and improve circulation, reducing congestion and inflammation in the eyes that can lead to swelling.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Pot Marigold plant contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing eye swelling.
Flowers: The flowers of the Pot Marigold plant are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate eye discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest calendula flowers at peak bloom for optimal potency and quality.
Dry calendula flowers in a low-temperature oven or dehydrator within 24 hours.
Chop dried calendula flowers into small pieces using scissors or mortar and pestle.
Combine one part dried calendula with three parts vodka in a glass jar, shaking every day for 14 days.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into dark glass bottles, discarding solids after 21 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower tinctures helps with eye swelling because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, derived from the plant's active compounds, such as kaempferol and quercetin. These natural flavonoids work to reduce puffiness and redness by calming irritated tissues and soothing swollen eyelids. The tincture also contains antihistamine-like compounds that help alleviate itchiness and discharge associated with eye allergies or irritations. As a result, Kansas coneflower tinctures provide effective relief from uncomfortable and unsightly eye swelling, allowing for clear and comfortable vision.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Kansas Coneflower's antioxidant compounds help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to eye swelling.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in Kansas Coneflower have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and redness in the eyes.
Immunomodulatory properties: Kansas Coneflower has been shown to modulate the immune response, which can help prevent excessive inflammation and swelling in the eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and flavonoids, the roots have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce eye swelling.
Seeds: Containing sesquiterpenes and polyphenols, the seeds exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities that may alleviate eye inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh echinacea angustifolia roots in the morning after dew has dried.
Cleanly chop the harvested roots into small pieces and spread on paper towels overnight.
Weigh out 500 grams of the air-dried root material and pack it into a clean glass jar.
Add 80% vodka to the jar, covering the plant material by at least 2 inches, then seal tightly.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 6 weeks shaking daily before straining.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tinctures helps with eye swelling because its extract contains flavonoids, which possess potent anti-inflammatory properties. The tincture's ability to reduce inflammation in the body is particularly beneficial for alleviating eye swelling caused by allergies, sinus pressure, or minor injuries. As a natural anti-inflammatory agent, it works by blocking the production of prostaglandins, which are chemicals responsible for causing blood vessels to dilate and lead to swelling. This natural remedy promotes healthy vision and provides relief from discomfort and puffiness associated with eye swelling.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant compounds help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, which can contribute to eye swelling.
Vasodilatory effects: The flavonoids present in Maidenhair Tree tinctures can dilate blood vessels, reducing swelling by improving circulation and reducing fluid buildup around the eyes.
Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginkgo biloba contains anti-inflammatory saponins that may help reduce inflammation and edema in the eye tissues, alleviating swelling and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots are used to make tinctures that effectively reduce eye inflammation and alleviate swelling.
Barks: Containing antimicrobial properties, barks help to combat infection and promote healing in cases of eye swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality ginkgo biloba leaves from a trusted source in late summer or early fall when they are most potent.
Clean and dry the harvested leaves using a clean cloth or paper towels to remove any dirt or debris.
Chop the dried leaves into small pieces to increase their surface area for extraction, then combine 20 grams of chopped leaves with 100 milliliters of 95% ethanol in a glass jar.
Steep the leaf-ethanol mixture in a cool dark place at room temperature for 6 weeks, shaking the jar daily to ensure even extraction and dissolution.
Strain the liquid through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle, then store the tincture in an amber-colored glass container with tight-fitting lid away from direct sunlight.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort tinctures helps with eye swelling because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The herb contains hyperforin, a compound that has been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate swelling in various parts of the body, including the eyes. When applied topically as an eye drop or added to a warm compress, St John's Wort tincture can help to soothe and calm the affected area, reducing puffiness and discomfort associated with eye swelling.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: St. John's Wort contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that exhibit anti-inflammatory activity, which helps to reduce swelling and discomfort in the eyes.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in St. John's Wort tinctures help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to eye inflammation and oxidative stress, reducing the risk of damage to the delicate tissues of the eye.

Vasodilatory effects: The bioactive compounds in St. John's Wort tinctures may help dilate blood vessels, improving circulation and reducing congestion in the eyes, which can contribute to swelling and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and alleviate eye swelling.
Flowers: Contain flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective for soothing eye swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried hypericum perforatum leaves and flowers in the summer months.
Chop 100g of the herb material into small pieces and combine it with 500ml of 80% ethanol vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking the jar daily to extract the bioactive compounds.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and retaining the tincture solution.
Fill glass eyedropper bottles with the final 20ml of tincture solution and label each bottle with date and ingredients.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe tinctures helps with eye swelling because it possesses anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that effectively reduce puffiness and redness. The aloe vera gel, when extracted and concentrated into a tincture form, can be easily absorbed into the skin around the eyes, targeting the delicate orbital area to calm and decongest blood vessels. This natural remedy gently relieves eye strain and discomfort, leaving the eyes feeling refreshed, revitalized, and free from swelling.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Aloe tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes by suppressing pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines.

Antioxidant: Aloe's antioxidant properties, including vitamin C and beta-carotene, protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to eye swelling and puffiness.
Soften and calm irritated tissues: Aloe tincture's mucilages and polysaccharides soothe and soften irritated tissues in the eyes, reducing redness and discomfort associated with swelling.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing swelling and inflammation in the eyes.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes contain gel-like substances that can help soothe and calm irritated eyes, reducing redness and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera leaves from mature plants for optimal potency and quality.
Cut the leaves into small pieces and soak them in a clean glass jar with 100 proof vodka.
Store the mixture at room temperature for four weeks shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass container discarding solids.
Transfer the resulting tincture to dark glass bottles and store in the refrigerator for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Burdock tinctures helps with eye swelling because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce puffiness and soothe irritated eyes. The root extract of burdock contains powerful antioxidants that help to neutralize free radicals, thereby reducing inflammation and promoting healthy blood flow to the delicate tissues around the eyes. Additionally, burdock's gentle diuretic properties help to drain excess fluids from the body, further alleviating water retention and swelling in the eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Burdock root contains powerful anti-inflammatory compounds that can reduce swelling and ease discomfort in the eyes by reducing inflammation.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in burdock tincture can help neutralize free radicals that may be contributing to eye swelling, promoting overall eye health and protecting against damage.

Astringent and antiseptic properties: Burdock's astringent and antiseptic compounds can help reduce inflammation and prevent infection by constricting blood vessels and killing bacteria or other microorganisms in the affected area.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the burdock plant have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort in the eyes.
Seeds: Burdock seeds contain antioxidants and flavonoids that can help soothe and calm irritated eyes, reducing swelling and promoting healthy vision.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried burdock roots and cut them into small pieces to facilitate drying.
Dry the root pieces in a single layer on paper bags or trays for 7-10 days.
Grind the dried burdock roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix one part of the ground burdock with two parts of high-proof vodka or brandy, transfer to glass jars, and label.
Store the tincture in a cool dark place for at least 6 weeks before straining and decanting into smaller bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Field horsetail tinctures helps with eye swelling because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's high concentration of antioxidants, such as flavonoids and saponins, work to reduce inflammation and soothe irritated tissues in the eyes. This natural remedy has been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of conjunctivitis, blepharitis, and other ocular conditions characterized by swelling, redness, and discharge. By applying field horsetail tincture to the affected area, users can experience rapid relief from discomfort and improve overall eye health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Horsetail tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling in the eyes by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals, alleviating redness and discomfort.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant compounds present in horsetail tincture neutralize free radicals that can contribute to eye inflammation, protecting the delicate tissues of the eyes and reducing oxidative stress.
Vasodilatory: Horsetail tincture's vasodilatory effects help improve blood circulation around the eyes, which can reduce swelling by promoting healthy drainage and flushing out excess fluids.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in silica, which helps to reduce inflammation and ease congestion associated with eye swelling.
Rhizomes: Containing anti-inflammatory compounds that can help to soothe and calm irritated eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh equisetum arvense plant material in the morning after dew has dried.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm dark place for at least 24 hours.
Crush the dried plant material into small pieces using mortar and pestle.
Mix one part crushed plant material with five parts 95% ethanol vodka in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to macerate in a cool dark place for 2-4 weeks before straining and filtering.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Resin oak tinctures helps with eye swelling because its unique combination of plant extracts and essential oils has natural anti-inflammatory properties. The resin's bioactive compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, work synergistically to reduce inflammation and alleviate congestion in the eyes. By penetrating deep into the affected areas, these compounds help to dissipate swelling, ease pressure, and promote healthy vision. As a result, herbal resin oak tinctures can provide fast and effective relief for those experiencing eye swelling due to various causes such as allergies, sinusitis, or injury.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Oak resin contains flavonoids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Antioxidant properties: Oak resin's antioxidant compounds help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to eye swelling and other vision problems.
Immunomodulatory properties: Oak resin's immunomodulating effects can help regulate the body's immune response, reducing excessive inflammation and promoting healthy eye function.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the resin oak plant are used to make medicinal tinctures for eye swelling due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce redness and swelling in the eyes.
Barks: The barks of the resin oak plant contain compounds that have been traditionally used to treat eye problems such as conjunctivitis and other inflammatory conditions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh galactites tomentosa leaves and flowers in the morning after dewfall for optimal potency.
Chop the plant material into small pieces and soak it in vodka or high-proof grain alcohol at a ratio of 1:4.
Steep the mixture in a glass container at room temperature for eight weeks, shaking daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into dark glass bottles, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid.
Store the finished tincture in a cool dark place for up to two years, using a dropper to administer 20-30 drops as needed for eye swelling relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yellow gentian tinctures helps with eye swelling because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce puffiness and ease discomfort. The herbal extract contains flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that inhibit pro-inflammatory enzymes, thereby reducing inflammation and swelling in the eyes. Additionally, yellow gentian's diuretic properties help to remove excess fluid from the body, further alleviating eye swelling and promoting a sense of clarity and visual comfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: The tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling and pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thereby alleviating eye inflammation.

Antioxidant property: The antioxidants present in the tincture neutralize free radicals, which helps to protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by inflammation, thus reducing swelling.
Vasodilatory property: The vasodilatory effects of the tincture help to dilate blood vessels, improving circulation and reducing pressure on the eye, which in turn reduces swelling.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the yellow gentian plant contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds, including alkaloids and glycosides, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce eye swelling.

Barks: The barks of the yellow gentian plant have been traditionally used to make medicinal tinctures for eye swelling due to their ability to reduce inflammation and promote blood circulation in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried gentiana lutea roots and chop them into small pieces.
Combine one part chopped root with one part vodka in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks shaking daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another glass container.
Store the final tincture in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for eye swelling are a natural remedy that helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate discomfort associated with puffy or swollen eyes. These liquid extracts of plants, such as peppermint, chamomile, and calendula, work by calming the skin and reducing redness, allowing for improved vision and reduced sensitivity to light. For example, peppermint tincture can be applied topically to reduce swelling and itching, while chamomile tincture helps to soothe and calm irritated eyes. This natural solution improves lives by providing a non-invasive and gentle way to address eye issues, reducing the need for harsh chemicals or prescription medications.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat eye swelling with herbal tinctures is to combine a few specific formulas that reduce inflammation and promote healing. Apply a few drops of calendula tincture directly onto the affected area to soothe and calm the skin. Follow up with a compress soaked in witch hazel tincture to shrink swollen tissues. Finally, dilute echinacea tincture with water and apply it around the eye to enhance the immune response and reduce puffiness.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to eye swelling that are treated with herbal tinctures are various skin issues, such as acne, eczema, and dermatitis. Herbal tinctures like tea tree oil, lavender oil, and chamomile extract have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help soothe irritated skin and reduce swelling. Additionally, herbal tinctures can also be used to treat conditions like rosacea, psoriasis, and insect bites, which may exhibit similar symptoms of inflammation and redness.
